message:
fix(parser): correct json_remove function arity to VariadicMin(1)

The json_remove() function was incorrectly classified as requiring at
least 2 arguments (VariadicMin(2)), but SQLite's json_remove(json)
with a single argument is valid — it simply returns the JSON unchanged.

Per SQLite documentation: "If no path arguments are provided, then
json_remove(X) returns a minified version of the input JSON value X."

This fix allows single-argument json_remove calls to pass semantic
validation without spurious "wrong number of arguments" errors.

Signature: json_remove(json [, path, ...])
- Minimum args: 1 (json only)
- Maximum args: unlimited (json + any number of paths)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
